title: Bat Removal & Exclusion Services in Park Slope, Brooklyn | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Park Slope, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat control for brownstones and historic homes. Licensed experts. Call today!
intro: Park Slope residents dealing with bats in their attics, chimneys, or walls need professional removal services that protect both their historic homes and local wildlife. Our bat exclusion specialists provide humane, effective solutions tailored to the unique architecture of Park Slope's brownstones and townhouses. We ensure complete bat removal with preventative measures to keep your home bat-free year-round.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has over 15 years of experience serving Park Slope's distinctive Victorian and Romanesque Revival homes, understanding the specific entry points and roosting habits common in this neighborhood. Our licensed technicians use humane exclusion methods that comply with all NYC wildlife regulations while protecting your property's architectural integrity.
localContext: Park Slope's tree-lined streets and proximity to Prospect Park create an ideal habitat for bats, particularly in the ornate cornices, roof peaks, and chimney spaces of century-old brownstones along 7th Avenue and Berkeley Place. The neighborhood's historic architecture, while beautiful, often features gaps and openings that provide perfect roosting spots for bat colonies seeking shelter.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Park Slope?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Park Slope typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your home's architecture. Historic brownstones may require additional care to preserve architectural details during exclusion work.

faqs.question: Are bats common in Park Slope brownstones?
faqs.answer: Yes, bats are relatively common in Park Slope due to the neighborhood's proximity to Prospect Park and the abundance of older buildings with potential entry points. The Little Brown Bat and Big Brown Bat species frequently roost in attics, behind shutters, and in chimney spaces of historic homes.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in NYC humane and legal?
faqs.answer: All bat removal in New York City must be humane and follow strict guidelines. We use exclusion methods that allow bats to leave naturally but prevent re-entry, never trapping or harming them. Bat removal is prohibited during maternity season (May-August) when flightless pups are present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Park Slope home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark staining around roof edges or vents, small droppings in attics or near entry points, and seeing bats emerge from your home at sunset. Park Slope homeowners often notice bats around chimneys, decorative cornices, or damaged roof tiles on their historic properties.
